serviceHero.headline: Auto Insurance
serviceHero.subline: Get the best rate on personal auto coverage. We compare 30+ carriers so you don't have to.

whatItCovers.headline: What Auto Insurance Covers

included.title: Liability Coverage
included.description: Pays for injuries and property damage you cause to others in an accident.

included.title: Collision Coverage
included.description: Pays to repair or replace your car after a collision, regardless of fault.

included.title: Comprehensive Coverage
included.description: Covers theft, vandalism, weather damage, fire, and other non-collision events.

included.title: Personal Injury Protection (PIP)
included.description: Required in NY. Pays medical bills for you and your passengers regardless of fault.

included.title: Uninsured Motorist
included.description: Protects you if you're hit by a driver without insurance or a hit-and-run.

included.title: Rental Reimbursement
included.description: Covers rental car costs while your vehicle is being repaired after a covered claim.

excluded.title: Mechanical Breakdown
excluded.description: Normal wear and maintenance is not covered by auto insurance.

excluded.title: Commercial Use
excluded.description: Personal auto policies do not cover vehicles used for commercial delivery or rideshare without endorsement.

whatItCovers.whoNeedsIt: Any licensed driver who owns or leases a vehicle in New York is required by law to carry auto insurance. NY minimum requirements include Bodily Injury Liability (25/50/10), PIP, and Uninsured Motorist coverage.

steps.title: Tell Us About Your Vehicle

steps.title: Share Your Driver Info
steps.description: All drivers on the policy, driving history, and current insurer.

steps.title: Get Your Options
steps.description: We present quotes from multiple carriers with coverage comparison.

rateFactors.headline: What Affects Your Auto Insurance Rate

factors.description: Accidents and violations in the past 3–5 years significantly increase your premium.
Luxury vehicles and sports cars cost more to insure than standard sedans.
Drivers under 25 and over 75 typically pay higher rates.
Dense urban areas like Brooklyn tend to have higher rates due to accident frequency.
In NY, insurers can use credit-based insurance scores to set rates.
Driving fewer miles annually can qualify you for low-mileage discounts.

tips: Bundle your auto and home insurance for 10–25% savings
Take a defensive driving course for an additional 10% discount
Raise your deductible to lower your premium
Ask about telematics/usage-based programs if you're a safe driver

items.question: What is the minimum auto insurance required in NY?
items.answer: New York requires Bodily Injury Liability (25/50/10), Personal Injury Protection ($50,000 minimum), and Uninsured Motorist coverage (25/50).

items.question: Can I insure a leased vehicle?
items.answer: Yes. Leased vehicles typically require full coverage plus gap insurance, which covers the remaining loan balance if the car is totaled.

items.question: How does multi-car discounts work?
items.answer: Insuring two or more vehicles on the same policy typically earns a 10–25% discount on each vehicle's premium.

items.question: Does my credit score affect my auto insurance rate in NY?
items.answer: Yes. NY allows carriers to use credit-based insurance scores as a rating factor. A better credit score generally means a lower premium.

items.question: What happens to my rate after an accident?
items.answer: An at-fault accident typically increases your rate for 3–5 years depending on the carrier. We can often find you a better rate by switching carriers after an accident.
